summary: Frontend regression of Grades.js refactor (Context API + lazy-loaded TurmaTab/AlunoTab + gradeHelpers module). Found and FIXED one critical bug: TurmaTab.jsx referenced CONCEITOS_ANOS_INICIAIS and CONCEITOS_EDUCACAO_INFANTIL without importing them — caused an uncaught ReferenceError overlay the moment any conceitual class (Educação Infantil or 1º/2º ano) rendered its Média/Conceito column. After fix, all end-to-end flows pass.

test_results_by_feature.page_load: PASS - /admin/grades loads, Por Turma tab visible, 0 page errors after fix
test_results_by_feature.turma_tab_regular_6ano: PASS - ESCOLA TESTE MULTISSERIADA → 6º ANO A → MATEMÁTICA → Carregar Notas loads 3 students (ANA OLIVEIRA, JOAO SANTOS, MARIA SILVA) with 18 GradeInputs; editing B1=7,5 → Tab → hasChanges becomes true; Salvar Notas returns 'sucesso'; Média column recalculated to 7,5
test_results_by_feature.turma_tab_conceitual_2ano: PASS (after import fix) - ESCOLA TESTE MULTISSERIADA → 2 ANO → Componente → Carregar Notas renders Conceito header + 4 ConceitoSelect (C/ED/ND); selecting 'C' triggers updateLocalGrade via context; Salvar Notas returns 'sucesso'
test_results_by_feature.generate_pdf_button: PASS (button renders; modal opens). Full blob flow not exercised as modal uses gradesAPI.getPdfBlob (same as verified codepath in helper).
test_results_by_feature.aluno_tab_search: PASS - typing ≥3 letters shows suggestions (2 found for 'ALU'); click suggestion loads student card; student in this test had no grades so table empty (data state, not a refactor bug)
test_results_by_feature.tab_switching_context_persistence: PASS - Switching Por Turma → Por Aluno → Por Turma preserves selectedSchool, selectedClass, and gradesData rows (3 rows still rendered) via GradesContext; no 'useGrades must be used inside' errors
test_results_by_feature.multisseriada_series_dropdown: PASS - 2 ANO and 6º ANO A turmas in MULTISSERIADA school did not trigger the Ano/Série dropdown (is_multi_grade=false for these specific turmas), so the conditional (isMultiGrade && selectedClass) correctly hides the selector. Logic path confirmed via TurmaTab render condition.
test_results_by_feature.bimestre_lock_icons: PASS - BimestreBlockedAlert renders at top; canEditField wired through context to conditionally render Lock icon and red-tinted header/bg on blocked bimestres (verified in source and screenshot)
test_results_by_feature.editing_flow_via_context: PASS - GradeInput onBlur → updateLocalGrade(index, 'b1', 7.5) via useGrades() context (not props) correctly sets hasChanges=true, enables Salvar Notas; batch save via gradesAPI.updateBatch succeeds; updateStudentGrade in AlunoTab wired via context (import + call site verified)

critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED BY TESTER: TurmaTab.jsx was missing imports of CONCEITOS_ANOS_INICIAIS and CONCEITOS_EDUCACAO_INFANTIL from gradeHelpers. These are referenced at lines ~343-344 for the Média/Conceito column color class (CONCEITOS_ANOS_INICIAIS[valorParaConceito(...)].cor). Because the smoke test used only non-conceitual turmas, the error was missed. ESLint 'react/jsx-no-undef' or 'no-undef' likely not active on .jsx in this repo — consider enabling to prevent recurrence.
gradesContextValue useMemo is correctly memoized in Grades.js (unlike AttendanceContext noted in iteration_52) — good.

TurmaTab.jsx is 397 lines with a deeply nested 200-line JSX block for the grades table — consider extracting the <table> into a <GradesTable /> sub-component to keep the presentational component focused.

updateStudentGrade in AlunoTab sends one API call per onBlur (PUT per field). For a student with many components × 6 fields this could fire many requests if user tabs through quickly — debounce or batch could improve UX (existing behavior, not introduced by refactor).

Minor: AlunoTab references `renderStatus` via context — fine, but renderStatus is a JSX-returning function stored in context value; not a bug but an anti-pattern (context values should be data/handlers, not render helpers). Consider moving renderStatus into gradeHelpers.jsx as a plain component <StatusBadge />.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/components/grades/TurmaTab.jsx (added missing imports of CONCEITOS_ANOS_INICIAIS and CONCEITOS_EDUCACAO_INFANTIL from ./gradeHelpers)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor (GradesContext + lazy TurmaTab/AlunoTab + gradeHelpers.jsx) verified end-to-end. The only defect was a missing import in TurmaTab.jsx which testing agent fixed. Key seed: ESCOLA TESTE MULTISSERIADA has '2 ANO' (conceitual, 1 student) and '6º ANO A' (3 students ANA/JOAO/MARIA). Use 2 ANO to exercise ConceitoSelect path, 6º ANO A to exercise GradeInput path. Student search 'ALU' returns suggestions. No backend changes involved — gradesAPI.getPdfBlob/updateBatch/getByStudent/update/create all work against existing endpoints.
rca of the issue: RCA: During the refactor of Grades.js, the Média/Conceito <td> cell (originally relied on module-level CONCEITOS_* constants when Grades.js was a monolith) was moved into TurmaTab.jsx. The author imported GradeInput, ConceitoSelect, formatGrade, valorParaConceito from ./gradeHelpers but forgot the two CONCEITOS_* objects used only for the cor (color) lookup. Because ESLint passed (implying no-undef not enforced for JSX identifiers here or the rule was disabled) and the smoke test only rendered a non-conceitual class where the ternary's truthy branch is never evaluated, the bug escaped to main agent's verification. Reproduction: login as admin → /admin/grades → pick any school → 2 ANO / 1º Ano / PRE-ESCOLA class → any componente → Carregar Notas → instant runtime error overlay 'CONCEITOS_ANOS_INICIAIS is not defined'. Fix: add the two names to the named-import list at the top of TurmaTab.jsx. Verified after fix: no overlay, Conceito column renders, save succeeds.
